questions.q: What is the primary objective of Organising?
opts: (B) To reduce costs and increase efficiency
(D) To increase the size of the organisation
questions.explanation: Organising is the process of deciding how tasks should be performed, and it aims to reduce costs and increase efficiency. This involves establishing a formal structure, dividing work among individuals, and assigning tasks to them.

questions.q: Which of the following is an advantage of Organising?
questions.explanation: Organising leads to better resource allocation, as tasks are assigned to individuals based on their skills and abilities, resulting in optimal utilisation of resources.

questions.q: What is the role of communication in Organising?
opts: (A) It helps to establish a formal structure
(C) It reduces costs and increases efficiency
(D) It increases the size of the organisation
questions.explanation: Communication plays a crucial role in Organising by facilitating coordination and control among individuals and departments, ensuring that tasks are performed according to plan.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a well-planned organisation?
questions.explanation: A well-planned organisation has well-defined goals and objectives, which serve as a guide for decision-making and task performance.

questions.q: What is the difference between planning and Organising?
opts: (A) Planning involves deciding what to do, while Organising involves how to do it
(B) Planning involves how to do it, while Organising involves deciding what to do
(C) Planning involves both deciding what to do and how to do it
(D) Organising involves both deciding what to do and how to do it
questions.explanation: Planning involves deciding what to do, while Organising involves determining how to do it, i.e., how tasks should be performed.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of Organising framework?
questions.explanation: There are three types of Organising frameworks: Hierarchical structure, Functional structure, and Divisional structure. Each of these frameworks has its own advantages and disadvantages.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a functional structure?
opts: (A) To increase the size of the organisation
(B) To reduce costs and increase efficiency
questions.explanation: A functional structure is designed to facilitate coordination and control among departments, ensuring that tasks are performed according to plan.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a divisional structure?
opts: (A) It is suitable for large organisations
(B) It is suitable for small organisations
(C) It is suitable for organisations with diverse products
(D) It is suitable for organisations with a single product
questions.explanation: A divisional structure is suitable for organisations with diverse products, as it allows for decentralisation and flexibility in decision-making.

questions.q: What is the role of a line manager in Organising?
questions.explanation: A line manager is responsible for assigning tasks to employees, ensuring that tasks are performed according to plan, and providing feedback and guidance to employees.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of staff organisation?
questions.explanation: A staff organisation may include consultants and advisors, who provide expert advice and guidance to the organisation.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a staff organisation?
opts: (A) To increase the size of the organisation
(B) To reduce costs and increase efficiency
(C) To provide expert advice and guidance
questions.explanation: A staff organisation provides expert advice and guidance to the organisation, helping to improve decision-making and task performance.

questions.q: Which of the following is an advantage of a matrix structure?
questions.explanation: A matrix structure is designed to improve communication and coordination among departments, ensuring that tasks are performed according to plan.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a matrix structure?
opts: (A) To increase the size of the organisation
(B) To reduce costs and increase efficiency
(C) To facilitate coordination and control among departments
questions.explanation: A matrix structure is designed to facilitate coordination and control among departments, ensuring that tasks are performed according to plan.

questions.q: Which of the following is a challenge of implementing a matrix structure?
questions.explanation: Implementing a matrix structure can be challenging due to the complexity of the structure, decreased flexibility, and conflicting roles and responsibilities among employees.

questions.q: What is the role of technology in Organising?
opts: (B) To facilitate coordination and control among departments
(D) To reduce costs and increase efficiency
questions.explanation: Technology plays a crucial role in Organising by improving communication and decision-making, facilitating coordination and control among departments, and reducing costs and increasing efficiency.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of the impact of technology on Organising?
questions.explanation: The use of technology, such as email and video conferencing, has improved communication and decision-making among employees and departments.

questions.q: What is the role of leadership in Organising?
opts: (B) To facilitate coordination and control among departments
(D) To reduce costs and increase efficiency
questions.explanation: Leadership plays a crucial role in Organising by providing vision and direction, facilitating coordination and control among departments, and reducing costs and increasing efficiency.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of the impact of leadership on Organising?
questions.explanation: A leader who provides clear vision and direction can improve communication and decision-making among employees and departments, leading to increased efficiency and effectiveness.
